message:
Complete Trusted Types compliance for thread and message list rendering

This commit wraps the remaining innerHTML assignments in renderThreads() and
renderMessages() with createTrustedHTML() to achieve complete Trusted Types
compliance across the entire viewer.

Changes:

1. Thread List Rendering (renderThreads, lines 526-539)
   - Wrapped thread title innerHTML with createTrustedHTML()
   - Wrapped thread meta innerHTML with createTrustedHTML()
   - Wrapped thread preview innerHTML with createTrustedHTML()
   - All values use escapeHtml() or are static HTML templates
   - Search highlighting via highlightText() also entity-escapes

2. Message List Rendering (renderMessages, lines 605-613)
   - Wrapped message title innerHTML with createTrustedHTML()
   - Wrapped message meta innerHTML with createTrustedHTML()
   - Wrapped message snippet innerHTML with createTrustedHTML()
   - Search term highlighting preserves entity escaping
   - Importance and timestamp values properly escaped

Coverage:

Now ALL innerHTML assignments in the entire viewer are Trusted Types compliant:
✓ Message detail header (commit 4b9e471)
✓ Message detail attachments (commit 4b9e471)
✓ Message detail body markdown (commit 19c099d)
✓ Thread list rendering (this commit)
✓ Message list rendering (this commit)

Behavior:

Before: Browser console errors "This document requires 'TrustedHTML' assignment"
After: All innerHTML operations succeed without CSP violations

Search highlighting continues to work because:
- highlightText() wraps matches in <mark> tags
- All text content is entity-escaped before wrapping
- <mark> tags are safe HTML, no XSS risk
- createTrustedHTML() validates the escaped HTML structure

The viewer now achieves 100% Trusted Types compliance and can run in the most
restrictive CSP environments without any console errors or security warnings.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
